title: planchonella sandwicensis
intro: Planchonella sandwicensis, also known as the Ohia lehua tree, is a flowering plant species native to Hawaii. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Hawaiian medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments. The bark and leaves of the Planchonella sandwicensis have been used to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often dried and made into teas or infusions, or used in topical applications to promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin.
